page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. The system demonstrates high memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read/write operations, alongside strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and ray tracing. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the n2-highcpu-96 offers a highly optimized compute-to-memory ratio for workloads that require massive parallel processing power without the overhead of large memory footprints. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces baseline costs for pure CPU-bound tasks, though workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware acceleration will need to attach external resources, affecting overall cost efficiency.

This server is designed for highly parallel, compute-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, ray tracing, static web serving, and database operations. It is also capable of handling moderate large language model prompt processing, though text generation speeds remain average.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This server does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, while single-core metrics remain average. This profile makes the n2-highcpu-96 highly cost-efficient for parallel, CPU-bound workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving that do not require massive memory allocations or local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and no bundled storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized server delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel, CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
96.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and software compilation benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for parallel processing, ray tracing, and static web serving
